title: Graham's Conjecture on Optimal Addressings
statement_text: $N_2(G) = n-1$ for almost all graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices.
context_text: In [7, page 148], Ron Graham writes that it is natural to guess that $N_2(G) = n-1$ for almost all connected graphs on $n$ vertices. Computations by the paper's authors on all connected graphs with up to 10 vertices contradicted this belief, motivating a proof of the opposite.
paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture by proving that $N_2(G) \leq n - (2 - o(1))\log_2 n$ for almost all connected graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices.
notes: Described as a 'suggested conjecture' in prose without a labelled theorem environment; attributed to [7, page 148].
paper_title: Addressing Johnson graphs, complete multipartite graphs, odd cycles and other graphs

title: Question of Watanabe, Ishii, and Sawa on N3 of odd cycles
statement_text: Does the formula for $N_3(C_{2n+1})$ established by Watanabe, Ishii, and Sawa hold for all (larger) values of $n$?
context_text: Watanabe, Ishii, and Sawa [14] proved a formula for $N_3(C_{2n+1})$ for small odd cycles and explicitly asked whether it extends to all $n$. The paper computes specific values $N_3(C_{11}), N_3(C_{13}), N_3(C_{15}), N_3(C_{17}), N_3(C_{19})$ to investigate this.
paper_contribution: Resolves the question: the formula is confirmed for $n \in \{6, 7, 8, 9\}$ (corresponding odd cycles $C_{13}, C_{15}, C_{17}, C_{19}$) but shown to fail for $n = 11$.
notes: PDF source — the exact formula for $N_3(C_{2n+1})$ from [14] is heavily garbled in the extraction; the precise index range for the true/false cases may also be garbled.
paper_title: Addressing Johnson graphs, complete multipartite graphs, odd cycles and other graphs
